candidate_problems.text: How can we distinguish a genuinely sociostructural bottleneck from the possibility that unification is mainly blocked by the objective hardness or even ill-posedness of the physics problem itself? The conjecture risks over-attributing stagnation to incentive structure unless it specifies observable signatures that separate coordination failure from intrinsic explanatory difficulty.

What institutional or epistemic mechanisms would actually increase the production of integrative explanatory work without merely subsidizing low-accountability speculation? The open problem is to design and test funding, training, evaluation, and collaboration structures that reward long-horizon unification efforts while preserving strong criticism and error-correction.

If unification is a public good, what counts as the relevant unit of progress and payoff, and how should integrative success be measured before a final theory exists? The conjecture raises an unresolved question about metrics: whether explanatory integration, cross-domain constraint satisfaction, recovery of limiting cases, or novel problem-reframing can be operationalized well enough to guide collective investment.
